competitor_patterns: Top-ranking content (road.cc, runbikecalc) uses explicit year in H1 and meta title ('Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026') combined with a clear benefit hook (UV, ventilation, lens clarity) — signals freshness and intent match simultaneously
Runbikecalc ranks #1 at only 1,063 words by leading with a 'TL;DR / Quick Verdict' section and a comparison table above the fold — satisfying informational intent fast without requiring deep scroll
All top-3 competitors include FAQ schema and structured Q&A sections, directly targeting People Also Ask boxes and AI Overview extraction points

Intheknow and Bikerumor anchor reviews to specific named products (Oakley Sutro, POC Elicit, Tifosi Crit) with hands-on test language ('I tested', 'Bikerumor Tested') — building E-E-A-T signals through first-person authority
Demonsun ranks #1 for 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' by making that single feature the entire page focus — tight topical relevance beats broad roundup pages for feature-specific queries
our_gaps: None of our recent posts target a named competitor brand directly in the H1 or meta title (e.g. 'vs Oakley', 'vs POC') — missing high-intent commercial comparison traffic that Demonsun and Bikerumor capture

Our posts lack a comparison table above the fold in the first 200 words — the 'Best Value' post has one but it appears mid-article; top competitors surface it immediately to reduce bounce from scanners

We have no content targeting the interchangeable lens feature as a standalone pillar page — our StradaPro advantage is buried inside broader posts rather than owning 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' as a dedicated URL

Our word counts (1,074–3,067) are inconsistent and none of our shorter posts compensate with strong structured data (product schema, review schema, HowTo schema) — road.cc's 13,959-word authority page is unbeatable on depth; our shorter posts need schema to compete

Our posts do not include real test/ride data, specific numbers (distance ridden, temperature range, lux conditions) or named test riders — missing the E-E-A-T signals that allow Google to trust us as a review source vs. a brand promotional page
keyword_opportunities: interchangeable lens cycling glasses — Demonsun ranks #1, we have zero dedicated content despite this being StradaPro's core USP; estimated commercial intent is high
lightweight cycling glasses under 30g — no competitor has a dedicated page targeting sub-30g weight as a primary keyword; our 25g frame is a direct match and differentiator
anti-fog cycling glasses road cycling — mentioned in passing by competitors but no top-3 page owns this as a primary topic; high relevance for sportive and early-morning riders
cycling glasses 30 day trial — zero competitor content targets risk-free trial as a search intent; captures bottom-funnel buyers comparing purchase confidence

best cycling glasses for long rides endurance — Bikerumor and road.cc cover 'best cycling sunglasses' broadly but no top page targets the endurance/sportive sub-segment specifically, where fit, anti-fog and weight are paramount
next_post_guidelines: Target one specific long-tail keyword per post — do not write another broad 'best cycling glasses 2026' article; instead pick one feature keyword (e.g. 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses') and make the entire post about that single topic with StradaPro as the answer
Put the TL;DR verdict and a comparison table in the first 300 words — mirror Runbikecalc's structure that ranked #1 at 1,063 words; scanners should know our conclusion before scrolling

Include specific, quantified test claims in every post — e.g. 'tested over 200km across 4 ride types', 'measured at 25g on a kitchen scale', 'lens swap under 8 seconds' — these are E-E-A-T signals and AI Overview extraction fodder

Add product schema markup (name, brand, weight, price range, aggregateRating) and FAQ schema to every post — with no GSC data yet, schema is the fastest way to appear in PAA boxes and AI Overviews before organic rank builds

Write one H2 per post that directly names the primary competitor in the target keyword's top result (e.g. 'How StradaPro Compares to Demonsun Interchangeable Lens Glasses') — this captures comparison intent from users already in the funnel

End every post with a clear CTA linking to the StradaPro product page and referencing the 30-day trial explicitly — convert informational readers; none of our recent posts make the trial the closing hook

Use first-person ride experience language ('On a 90-minute morning ride in fog, the anti-fog system held without a single patch') — brand blogs that read as test reports outperform those that read as spec sheets for E-E-A-T signals
seo_quick_wins: Add FAQ schema (JSON-LD) to every existing and future post immediately — all top-3 competitors use it, it takes under 30 minutes per post, and it is the single fastest path to People Also Ask and AI Overview presence with a new domain

Rewrite every meta title to follow the formula: '[Primary Keyword] [Year]: [Specific Benefit or Differentiator] | Velluto' — current titles like 'Cycling Glasses Better Value Than Alibaba 2026 | Velluto' bury the benefit; lead with the keyword and a concrete hook within 60 characters

Add an internal link cluster: every post must link to the StradaPro product page AND to at least two other blog posts using descriptive anchor text matching target keywords — with a new domain, internal linking is critical for passing crawl equity and establishing topical authority before external links exist
meta_title_fixes: No GSC data available yet — meta title fix recommendations cannot be prioritised by CTR or impression volume. Apply the following pre-emptive fixes based on competitor title analysis: 'Cycling Glasses Better Value Than Alibaba 2026 | Velluto' → New title: 'Best Cycling Glasses Under €100: Skip Alibaba (2026)' | Why: leads with value intent keyword, removes brand-specific jargon 'Alibaba' that few users search, adds price signal that drives clicks

No GSC data available yet — 'Best Value Cycling Glasses 2026: Bing vs Velluto | Velluto' → New title: 'Best Value Cycling Glasses 2026: Full Comparison' | Why: 'Bing' as a competitor name is unrecognised to most cycling audiences; removing it and using 'Full Comparison' matches navigational + commercial intent

No GSC data available yet — 'Are Expensive Cycling Sunglasses Worth It? 2026 Guide | Velluto' → New title: 'Are Expensive Cycling Sunglasses Worth It? (2026)' | Why: already well-structured; trim '| Velluto' to stay under 60 chars and let the question format drive CTR from curiosity intent searches
content_quick_wins: No GSC data available yet — no near-top-3 keywords can be identified from impression data. No GSC data available yet — once GSC data is available (typically 4–6 weeks post-launch), re-run this analysis to identify queries ranking positions 4–15 for targeted content depth expansion

No GSC data available yet — monitor 'road cycling glasses review', 'lightweight cycling glasses', and 'anti-fog cycling sunglasses' as seed queries in GSC from day one; these align with StradaPro's documented advantages and have clear competitor content gaps identified above
geo_angles: Structure every post with a direct answer in the first sentence that names the product, its key spec, and a use-case — e.g. 'The Velluto StradaPro (25g, interchangeable lenses, €X) is the lightest cycling glass with a risk-free 30-day trial available in 2026' — AI Overviews and Perplexity pull the most entity-dense, factually specific opening sentences as answer snippets

Create a dedicated 'Velluto StradaPro: Full Specs & Review' page with structured data (Product schema, AggregateRating, FAQPage) that consolidates all product facts in one crawlable URL — ChatGPT and Perplexity prioritise pages that answer 'what is X' comprehensively in a single source, making this the highest-leverage AI-visibility page on the site

Publish a 'Cycling Glasses Buyer's Guide 2026' pillar page (2,500+ words) that covers every major decision criterion (weight, lens type, anti-fog, UV rating, fit, price) and positions StradaPro as the answer for each — broad, authoritative guide pages are disproportionately cited in AI-generated answers because they cover multiple subtopics that match diverse user queries in a single trusted source
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR Runbikecalc (ranks #1 'best cycling glasses 2026'): angle — 'The Glasses Runbikecalc Didn't Test (But Should Have)' — headline idea: 'Velluto StradaPro vs Oakley Sutro 2026: The 25g Challenger the Roundups Missed' | key differentiator: Runbikecalc tests only established brands with no new entrants; Velluto advantage to lead with: 25g weight vs Sutro's 36g + 30-day trial vs no-return-policy premium pricing

COMPETITOR Demonsun (ranks #1 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses'): angle — 'Interchangeable Lenses Done Right: Tool-Free Click-In vs Demon's Screw System' — headline idea: 'Velluto StradaPro vs Demon Sunglasses: Which Interchangeable Lens System is Actually Faster?' | key differentiator: Demon requires tools or fumbling; Velluto advantage to lead with: tool-free click-in swap timed in seconds, Italian design aesthetic, anti-fog system absent from Demonsun

COMPETITOR Intheknow (ranks #1 'road cycling glasses review'): angle — 'The Independent Review Test vs The Brand That Offers You 30 Days to Decide Yourself' — headline idea: 'Velluto StradaPro Review: We Give You 30 Days on Real Rides Instead of One Lab Opinion' | key differentiator: Intheknow is a single reviewer's subjective pick; Velluto advantage to lead with: 30-day risk-free trial turns every buyer into their own reviewer, removing the trust gap that editorial reviews fill
